page: Amazon Web Services r8a.medium is a memory-optimized virtual machine instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features a single dedicated physical core and vCPU from an AMD EPYC 9R45 processor operating at 4.5 GHz. The x86_64 architecture includes 8.0 GB of DDR5 memory running at 6 GHz, yielding a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8 GB per core. The processor is equipped with 48 KB L1d cache, 1.0 MB L2 cache, and 4 MB L3 cache, alongside advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512, AVX_VNNI, and cryptographic acceleration via AES and SHA_NI. No local NVMe storage or GPU accelerators are bundled with this instance, and baseline network bandwidth is rated at 1 Gbps.

Benchmark results reveal a stark contrast between single-core and multi-core performance due to the single-vCPU configuration. The instance achieves top-tier status in single-core metrics, including stress-ng single-core CPU performance, Geekbench single-core score, and single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression. However, multi-core benchmarks, such as stress-ng multi-core, Geekbench multi-core, and software compilation, fall into the bottom 25% tier. Memory bandwidth for large blocks is average, while small-block cached read bandwidth is weak.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the r8a.medium offers a modern DDR5 memory subsystem and high single-threaded performance at a low entry point for the r8a family, but it presents clear resource density tradeoffs. The lack of local storage and the single-vCPU limit make it poorly suited for parallel processing, ray tracing, or heavy database operations, as evidenced by weak Passmark database and Redis SET benchmarks. It is best suited for lightweight, memory-intensive workloads that require strong single-threaded execution, such as basic text processing, static web serving, or small-scale LLM inference prompt processing.
description: Amazon Web Services r8a.medium is a memory-optimized instance featuring a single dedicated AMD EPYC 9R45 vCPU running at 4.5 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. It is equipped with 8.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory operating at 6 GHz, but lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, the instance delivers top-tier single-core performance, as demonstrated by high stress-ng and Geekbench single-core scores, alongside strong single-threaded compression capabilities. However, its single-vCPU constraint limits multi-core and parallel processing efficiency, placing its multi-core, database, and Redis benchmarks in the lower tiers. This instance is qualitatively cost-effective for memory-demanding workloads with low concurrency requirements. It is best suited for single-threaded applications, static web serving, text processing, and lightweight memory-intensive tasks.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r8a.medium is a memory-optimized instance featuring 1 AMD EPYC vCPU, 8.0 GB of DDR5 RAM, and Nitro hypervisor virtualization, delivering strong single-core performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r8a.medium is a single-vCPU memory-optimized instance with 8.0 GB DDR5 RAM, offering top-tier single-core performance.
tagline: A single-core, memory-optimized virtual server delivering high-speed DDR5 memory and exceptional single-threaded performance for lightweight workloads.
bullet_points: Single dedicated AMD EPYC 9R45 vCPU running at 4.5 GHz
8.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory at 6 GHz
Top-tier single-core CPU and single-threaded compression performance
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
1 Gbps baseline network bandwidth powered by AWS Nitro
Best suited for static web serving, text processing, and light memory-intensive tasks
